choices.text: Required by FAR 91.103: weather (METAR/TAF/PIREP/SIGMET), fuel, alternates, performance, runway lengths, NOTAMs/TFRs, traffic delays. aviationweather.gov + ForeFlight standard tools.

METAR = current routine weather report at airport, hourly. TAF = 24-30 hour forecast at airport. PIREP = real pilot observations. SIGMET/AIRMET = hazardous weather. All in standardized aviation codes.
VFR day 30 min reserve, night 45 min (FAR 91.151). IFR 45 min plus alternate when destination forecast marginal (FAR 91.167).
Broken (5/8-7/8 sky covered) at 1,300 ft AGL. Code in hundreds of feet. FEW <2/8, SCT 3-4/8, BKN 5-7/8, OVC 8/8. Critical for VFR ceiling determination.
Density altitude check: hot + high + humid = poor performance. POH charts for takeoff distance vs. DA + weight + wind + surface. Add 50% safety margin. Consider lighter load + cooler morning.
